description: Fix unsafe (data as any) casts in create-tweet.ts that bypass TypeScript type safety, causing silent runtime failures if the X API response shape changes.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/x && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace (data as any) casts in create-tweet.ts with typed response interface
userStories.description: In plugins/x/src/tools/create-tweet.ts, the handle function casts the graphqlMutation response to `(data as any)` on lines 39-41 to access `data.data.create_tweet.tweet_results.result` and `data.errors`. These any-casts silence TypeScript, so if the X API changes the response shape, the tool silently returns undefined instead of a structured error. Define a typed interface for the CreateTweet GraphQL response and use it instead.
acceptanceCriteria: No (data as any) casts remain in create-tweet.ts
The CreateTweet GraphQL response shape is expressed as a TypeScript interface
graphqlMutation is called with the response type parameter so TypeScript validates the access paths
The errors field access is type-safe (no any casts)
